title: DocuSign secret keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: DocuSign Integration Key detected in variable assignment. DocuSign integration keys (also called client IDs) identify your DocuSign application and are used for OAuth authentication. Exposing this key combined with client secrets or private keys allows attackers to impersonate your application, generate unauthorized eSignature requests, access signed documents, and send fraudulent contracts to users. Revoke immediately in the DocuSign Developer Console.

DocuSign API credential detected near DocuSign keywords. DocuSign API keys and secrets authenticate access to eSignature workflows, document management, and contract execution. Exposing these credentials allows unauthorized sending of legal documents, access to signed contracts, and manipulation of envelope workflows. Revoke at admindemo.docusign.com or app.docusign.com.

checks.pattern: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----[\s\S]*?-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
checks.message: RSA Private Key detected near DocuSign context. DocuSign uses RSA private keys for JWT Grant authentication flow to obtain access tokens. Exposing the RSA private key used with DocuSign allows attackers to impersonate your application, obtain access tokens, and perform all DocuSign operations your integration is authorized for, including sending, voiding, and accessing signed legal documents.

DocuSign JWT token detected. DocuSign JWT tokens provide time-limited access to the eSignature API. Exposing a valid JWT token allows unauthorized API operations during the token's validity period including creating envelopes, sending documents for signature, accessing completed documents, and viewing account information.
